paper_title: Stochastic Hamiltonian Model for Molecular Vibrational Motion

methods.description: This method involves constructing a stochastic sequence to model the stochastic force F(t) as a series of random impulses, which is crucial for simulating the vibrational motion of molecules under the influence of white shot noise.

steps.description: Assume that the random times t_i form a Poisson sequence of points.
steps.searchable_summary: generate Poisson sequence for random times t_i

steps.description: Generate random variables γ_i and t_i as mutually uncorrelated using distribution functions.
steps.searchable_summary: generate uncorrelated random variables γ_i and t_i
inputs: distribution functions for γ_i and t_i
outputs: uncorrelated random variables γ_i and t_i

steps.description: Construct the stochastic force F(t) as a sequence (γ_i, t_i).
steps.searchable_summary: construct stochastic force F(t) from sequence (γ_i, t_i)

steps.description: Calculate the power spectrum of the stochastic force F(t)F(s) using Fourier transform.
steps.searchable_summary: compute power spectrum of F(t)F(s) via Fourier transform
